binaries.title: pci.sys (Windows PCI Bus Driver) - Missing MSI-X Capability Validation and Dangling IO-Mapping Pointer causing Double MmUnmapIoSpace

changed_functions.name: PciProcessInterruptDescriptor
key_changes: Patched version moves the MSI-X capability read (PciReadDeviceConfig) to BEFORE the pool allocation and adds validation: the capability ID byte must equal 0x11 (PCI_CAP_ID_MSIX) and the table BIR field (& 7) must not equal 7 (i.e. must be <= 6). On failure it returns STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST (0xC0000184).
Unpatched version reads the MSI-X capability AFTER allocating and copying, and stores the device-supplied BIR (BYTE4 & 7) and table offset without validating the capability ID or bounding the BIR.

The config-read offset field moved from device_ext+0x39 (unpatched) to device_ext+0x3D (patched) purely because the device-extension layout shifted between builds; both hold the stored MSI-X capability offset (capability ID 0x11 is recorded there during enumeration).
ExAllocatePoolWithTag (POOL_FLAG_NON_PAGED, tag 0x42696350) replaced with ExAllocatePool2 (flag 0x40, tag 0x42696350) - API modernization.
Adds state flag: _InterlockedOr64(device_ext+0x6b0, 0x2000000000000000) in the success paths - state tracking, not a security check.

No integer-overflow fix: the 32-bit allocation-size computation 'imul eax, [rsi], 0x58; add eax, 8' at 0x1c004d2a9 and the memmove length (r13d, equal to the allocation size) are byte-for-byte identical in both builds and are not touched by the patch.
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched PciProcessInterruptDescriptor processes MSI-X table BAR information and reads the device's MSI-X capability from PCI configuration space AFTER allocation, storing the device-supplied BIR (BAR index, low 3 bits) and table offset without checking that the capability is genuinely MSI-X (ID 0x11) or that the BIR is in range. The patched version reads the capability BEFORE allocation and validates (1) capability ID byte == 0x11 and (2) BIR field (& 7) != 7, returning STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST otherwise. This defends against a malicious or malformed PCIe device that presents a crafted or mutated MSI-X capability (for example by changing its config space between enumeration and device start). The unchecked BIR and table offset otherwise flow into the BAR-to-vector setup that PciProcessStartResources feeds to MmMapIoSpaceEx, so an out-of-range value mis-directs an IO-space mapping within the device's assigned resource set. Reachable only during PnP device start / enumeration on the device's own config space, not from an untrusted IOCTL surface. NOTE: there is no integer overflow here - the 88*count+8 allocation-size multiply is 32-bit in both builds and the memmove copies exactly the allocation size in both builds (matched size, no overflow), and the patch does not modify this arithmetic.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- UNPATCHED PciProcessInterruptDescriptor 0x1C004D10C (MSI-X path, no validation) ---
1C004D22D: mov     dword ptr [rbx], 3            ; type = MSI-X
1C004D233: test    r9d, r9d
1C004D236: jz      1C004D24D                     ; proceed to allocation
1C004D2A9: imul    eax, [rsi], 58h               ; size = count*0x58 (32-bit) - identical in patched
1C004D2B4: add     eax, 8
1C004D2B9: mov     r13d, eax                      ; same value reused as copy length
1C004D2BC: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
1C004D2D4: mov     r8d, r13d                      ; Size = allocation size (NOT larger)
1C004D2DD: call    memmove                        ; copy length == allocation size (no overflow)
1C004D2F9: call    PciReadDeviceConfig            ; MSI-X cap read AFTER alloc, no validation
1C004D302: and     eax, 0FFFFFFF8h                ; table offset
1C004D30C: and     al, 7                          ; BIR stored unchecked
1C004D30E: mov     [rbx+8], al

--- PATCHED PciProcessInterruptDescriptor 0x14009C810 (added validation) ---
14009C89F: mov     dword ptr [rbx], 3            ; type = MSI-X
14009C8AE: movzx   r8d, byte ptr [rdi+3Dh]       ; MSI-X cap offset (relocated device-ext field)
14009C8B3: lea     r9d, [rcx+0Bh]
14009C8BF: call    PciReadDeviceConfig            ; read BEFORE alloc
14009C8C4: cmp     byte ptr [rsp+var_68], 11h     ; capability ID must be MSI-X (0x11)
14009C8C9: jnz     14009C9B8                      ; reject
14009C8CF: mov     eax, dword ptr [rsp+var_68+4]
14009C8D3: and     eax, 7
14009C8D6: cmp     al, 6                          ; table BIR must be <= 6
14009C8D8: ja      14009C9B8                      ; reject
14009CA17: mov     eax, 0C0000184h                ; STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST

changed_functions.name: PciProcessStartResources
key_changes: Patched version adds *(arg5 + 0x10) = 0 after MmUnmapIoSpace to clear the stored IO-space mapping pointer, preventing a later DOUBLE MmUnmapIoSpace on the same VA.

The MSI-X descriptor (arg5) is device_ext+0x388 (persistent in the device extension); arg5+0x10 = device_ext+0x398 = the mapped BaseAddress. The teardown paths PciDevice_Remove (0x1C0010340) and PciDevice_Stop (0x1C006CAE0) re-unmap device_ext+0x398 if it is non-NULL, so leaving it stale causes a second unmap of an already-freed system-PTE range.
Patched version adds a legacy VGA resource filter (PciIsVgaResource, 0x14009C7BC) and a second output buffer - refactor.
Struct offset changes: 0x414 -> 0x524 (BAR count), 0x410 -> 0x520 (MSI type), 0x1ec -> 0x2fc (window descriptor base), 0x38c -> 0x49c (interrupt pin).
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched PciProcessStartResources maps the MSI-X table BAR via MmMapIoSpaceEx (0x1c004d0df) and stores the VA at device_ext+0x398 (the +0x10 field of the persistent MSI-X descriptor at device_ext+0x388). On the error/cleanup path it calls MmUnmapIoSpace (0x1c0063839) but does NOT clear device_ext+0x398. Because the descriptor is persistent, the stale VA survives the failed device start, and the teardown paths PciDevice_Remove (0x1C0010340) and PciDevice_Stop (0x1C006CAE0) then unmap device_ext+0x398 a SECOND time (they check device_ext+0x388 == 3 and unmap device_ext+0x398 if non-NULL). The result is a double MmUnmapIoSpace of the same, already-unmapped system-PTE mapping - a dangling/expired-pointer bug leading to a bugcheck or system-PTE accounting corruption. The patched version adds *(device_ext+0x398) = 0 immediately after the first MmUnmapIoSpace (0x14009c72d), so teardown skips the second unmap. Reachable only during PnP device start / enumeration on the device's own config space; the trigger is an MSI-X resource-processing failure partway through start (invalid interrupt descriptor or a failed MmMapIoSpaceEx) followed by device stop/remove. No demonstrated controlled read/write primitive.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- UNPATCHED PciProcessStartResources 0x1C004CE40 (dangling pointer) ---
1C004D0DF: call    cs:__imp_MmMapIoSpaceEx        ; map physical IO space
1C004D0EB: mov     [rbp+10h], rax                 ; store VA into device_ext+0x398
; --- error/cleanup site ---
1C0063818: mov     rcx, [rsp+arg_20]              ; arg5 = device_ext+0x388
1C0063820: cmp     dword ptr [rcx], 3             ; MSI-X?
1C0063829: mov     rcx, [rcx+10h]                 ; BaseAddress (device_ext+0x398)
1C0063830: jz      1C004CF6C
1C0063836: mov     edx, r13d
1C0063839: call    cs:__imp_MmUnmapIoSpace
1C0063840: nop
1C0063845: nop                                    ; *** no clear of device_ext+0x398 ***
1C0063846: jmp     1C004CF6C

--- PATCHED PciProcessStartResources 0x14009C500 (clears pointer) ---
14009C708: cmp     dword ptr [rsi], 3            ; rsi = device_ext+0x388
14009C711: mov     rcx, [rsi+10h]                ; BaseAddress (device_ext+0x398)
14009C71E: mov     edx, r13d
14009C721: call    cs:__imp_MmUnmapIoSpace
14009C72D: mov     qword ptr [rsi+10h], 0        ; *** CLEAR device_ext+0x398 (FIX) ***
14009C735: jmp     14009C678

--- TEARDOWN re-unmap (present in both builds), e.g. PciDevice_Remove 0x1C0010340 ---
if (*(device_ext+0x388) == 3) { va = *(device_ext+0x398); if (va) { MmUnmapIoSpace(va, 16*BAR_count); *(device_ext+0x398)=0; } }

changed_functions.name: PciDeviceStartWorker
key_changes: Caller of PciProcessStartResources - PCI device start / resource processing function.
Processes the PnP IRP for device start; passes device_ext+0x388 as the persistent MSI-X output descriptor (arg5).
Struct offset changes between versions (0x414 -> 0x524 etc.).
Passes an additional output buffer parameter in the patched version.

changed_functions.name: PciAssignSlotResources
key_changes: Second caller of PciProcessStartResources - slot resource assignment path.
Significantly rewritten between versions due to struct layout changes.
Calls PciProcessStartResources with a CmResourceList during resource assignment.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: improper validation of device-controlled config data
security_findings.description: On the MSI-X path (device-extension MSI type field == 3), the unpatched PciProcessInterruptDescriptor reads the device's MSI-X capability from PCI configuration space AFTER allocation and stores the device-supplied table BIR (BAR index, low 3 bits) and table offset without checking that the capability ID byte is 0x11 (PCI_CAP_ID_MSIX) or that the BIR is in range. The patched version moves the PciReadDeviceConfig read to BEFORE the allocation and validates: (1) capability ID byte == 0x11 and (2) BIR field (& 7) != 7 (i.e. <= 6). If either check fails it returns STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST (0xC0000184). The config-read offset field moved from device_ext+0x39 (unpatched) to device_ext+0x3D (patched) only because the device-extension layout shifted; both hold the stored MSI-X capability offset. There is NO integer overflow: the 32-bit allocation-size computation 'imul eax, [rsi], 0x58; add eax, 8' at 0x1c004d2a9 and the memmove length are identical in both builds (the copy length equals the allocation size), and the patch does not change this arithmetic.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: A malicious or malformed PCIe device (physical, Thunderbolt/USB4, or VM PCI passthrough / SR-IOV) advertises an MSI-X capability so the device's MSI type field is set to MSI-X during enumeration. On IRP_MN_START_DEVICE, the device presents an MSI-X capability whose ID byte != 0x11 or whose table BIR field == 7 (for example by mutating config space after enumeration). The unpatched driver proceeds without validation and feeds the device-supplied BIR / table offset into the BAR-to-vector setup consumed by PciProcessStartResources; the patched driver rejects the start with STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST. Reachable only during PnP device start / enumeration on the device's own config space, not from an untrusted IOCTL surface.
security_findings.primitive: Device-controlled MSI-X BIR selection and table offset flow into MmMapIoSpaceEx parameters. The mapped physical base comes from the OS-assigned CmResourceList; the device controls only which resource descriptor is selected (BIR) and the table offset added to that base, bounded to its assigned resource set. This is a validation / robustness fix (and defends against a config-space time-of-check/time-of-use by a device that mutates its capability between enumeration and start). No demonstrated pool overflow, arbitrary mapping, or read/write primitive.
security_findings.evidence:
// UNPATCHED PciProcessInterruptDescriptor - MSI-X path, cap read AFTER alloc, no validation
1C004D22D: mov     dword ptr [rbx], 3            ; type = MSI-X
1C004D2A9: imul    eax, [rsi], 58h               ; size = count*0x58 (32-bit); identical in patched
1C004D2B9: mov     r13d, eax                      ; same value reused as copy length
1C004D2BC: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
1C004D2D4: mov     r8d, r13d                      ; copy length == allocation size (no overflow)
1C004D2DD: call    memmove
1C004D2F9: call    PciReadDeviceConfig            ; cap read AFTER alloc
1C004D30C: and     al, 7                          ; BIR stored unchecked
1C004D30E: mov     [rbx+8], al

// PATCHED PciProcessInterruptDescriptor - validate BEFORE alloc
14009C8AE: movzx   r8d, byte ptr [rdi+3Dh]       ; MSI-X cap offset (relocated field)
14009C8BF: call    PciReadDeviceConfig            ; read BEFORE alloc
14009C8C4: cmp     byte ptr [rsp+var_68], 11h     ; must be MSI-X capability ID
14009C8C9: jnz     14009C9B8                      ; reject
14009C8D6: cmp     al, 6                          ; BIR must be <= 6
14009C8D8: ja      14009C9B8                      ; reject
14009CA17: mov     eax, 0C0000184h                ; STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST
security_findings.assembly:
PciProcessInterruptDescriptor MSI-X path (unpatched 0x1C004D10C):
1C004D14D: test    cl, 2
1C004D150: jz      1C004D1F7                      ; not an interrupt descriptor
1C004D17B: mov     ecx, [rdi+410h]                ; MSI type from device ext
1C004D181: sub     ecx, 2
1C004D21C: cmp     ecx, 1                          ; == 1 means MSI type 3 (MSI-X)
1C004D21F: jnz     1C006386F
1C004D225: cmp     ebp, [rdx]
1C004D227: jnb     1C0063879
1C004D22D: mov     dword ptr [rbx], 3             ; type = MSI-X
1C004D233: test    r9d, r9d
1C004D236: jz      1C004D24D
1C004D287: mov     r8d, [rdi+414h]                ; BAR count
1C004D290: shl     r8, 2
1C004D294: mov     rcx, rax
1C004D297: call    memset
1C004D2A9: imul    eax, [rsi], 58h               ; 32-bit size (identical in patched)
1C004D2B4: add     eax, 8
1C004D2B9: mov     r13d, eax                      ; reused as copy length
1C004D2BC: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
1C004D2D4: mov     r8d, r13d                      ; Size = allocation size
1C004D2D7: mov     rdx, rsi                       ; Src
1C004D2DD: call    memmove                        ; copy length == allocation size
1C004D2EB: movzx   r8d, byte ptr [rdi+39h]        ; MSI-X cap offset
1C004D2F0: mov     r9d, 0Ch
1C004D2F9: call    PciReadDeviceConfig            ; cap read AFTER alloc, no validation
1C004D302: and     eax, 0FFFFFFF8h                ; table offset
1C004D305: mov     [rbx+0Ch], eax
1C004D30C: and     al, 7                          ; BIR stored unchecked
1C004D30E: mov     [rbx+8], al
1C004D311: jmp     1C004D238

breakpoints.why: Entry of the MSI/MSI-X interrupt descriptor processor. RCX=arg1 (device extension), RDX=arg2 (resource list, [rdx]=descriptor count), R8=arg3 (descriptor), R9D=arg4 (index), [rsp+0xb0]=arg5 (MSI-X output descriptor = device_ext+0x388).

Unpatched: PciReadDeviceConfig reads the MSI-X capability AFTER allocation, and the BIR/table offset are stored without validation. In the patched build the read+validation happen before allocation at 0x14009c8bf-0x14009c8d8.

Patched capability ID check: cmp byte [rsp+var_68], 0x11 (must be MSI-X). The following cmp al,6 (0x14009c8d6) bounds the table BIR. Failure jumps to 0x14009c9b8 which returns STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST (0xC0000184).

watch: RSI (arg2) = resource list pointer, [RSI] = descriptor count - drives the 88*count+8 allocation size (32-bit, identical in both builds; copy length equals allocation size)
[rsp+var_68] after PciReadDeviceConfig (patched) = capability bytes: low byte = capability ID (expect 0x11), BYTE4 & 7 = BIR (rejected if 7)
device_ext+0x410 (unpatched) / +0x520 (patched) = MSI type field (3 = MSI-X path)
key_offsets: 0x1c004d22d: mov dword [rbx], 3 - MSI-X type set (no pre-validation, unpatched)
0x1c004d2a9: imul eax, [rsi], 0x58 - 32-bit size compute (identical in both builds)
0x1c004d2dd: call memmove - copy length equals allocation size (no overflow)
0x1c004d2f9: call PciReadDeviceConfig - cap read AFTER alloc, unchecked (unpatched)
0x14009c8bf: call PciReadDeviceConfig - cap read BEFORE alloc (patched)
0x14009c8c4: cmp byte [rsp+var_68], 0x11 - MSI-X capability ID check (patched)
0x14009c8d6: cmp al, 6 - table BIR upper bound (patched)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Reach via PnP device start: the code runs when the PnP Manager sends IRP_MN_START_DEVICE to a PCI device. In a VM with PCI passthrough (Hyper-V Discrete Device Assignment) or SR-IOV, attach a device (or emulate one) that advertises an MSI-X capability during enumeration and then presents an MSI-X capability with an invalid ID byte (!= 0x11) or table BIR field == 7 at start (e.g. by mutating config space). Trigger device enumeration/start.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: the driver continues device start and stores the unchecked BIR at descriptor +0x8, feeding it into the later MmMapIoSpaceEx setup in PciProcessStartResources. Patched: PciProcessInterruptDescriptor returns 0xC0000184 (STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST) and does not allocate, so device start fails cleanly.
debugging_aids.notes: PCI capability ID 0x11 = MSI-X. The table BIR (BAR indicator) is the low 3 bits of the MSI-X Table Offset/BIR register; valid BAR indices are 0-5 (six standard BARs) with 6 sometimes used, so the patched check rejects only 7. Pool tag is 0x42696350 ('PciB'). The function is called from PciProcessStartResources (0x1c004ce40) at call site 0x1c004cff8, which is called from PciDeviceStartWorker (0x1c004c480). Device extension offsets (unpatched): +0x410 MSI type, +0x414 BAR count, +0x38c interrupt pin, +0x39 MSI-X cap offset; the same fields are at +0x520, +0x524, +0x49c, +0x3d in the patched build.

call_chain: PciProcessStartResources (CmResourceList descriptor iterator and MSI-X BAR mapper, 0x1c004ce40)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: expired pointer dereference leading to double unmap
security_findings.description: The unpatched PciProcessStartResources calls MmMapIoSpaceEx (0x1c004d0df) to map the MSI-X table BAR and stores the VA at device_ext+0x398 (the +0x10 field of the persistent MSI-X descriptor at device_ext+0x388). On the error/cleanup path it calls MmUnmapIoSpace (0x1c0063839) but does NOT clear device_ext+0x398. Because the descriptor is persistent in the device extension, the stale VA survives the failed start. The device teardown paths PciDevice_Remove (0x1c0010340) and PciDevice_Stop (0x1c006cae0) then re-check device_ext+0x388 == 3 and unmap device_ext+0x398 a SECOND time. The result is a double MmUnmapIoSpace of the same, already-unmapped system-PTE range. The patched version adds 'mov qword [rsi+0x10], 0' (0x14009c72d) immediately after MmUnmapIoSpace to clear the pointer so teardown skips the second unmap.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. An MSI-X-capable PCI device is started (IRP_MN_START_DEVICE), causing MmMapIoSpaceEx to map the MSI-X table BAR into kernel virtual memory (device_ext+0x398 set). 2. MSI-X resource processing fails partway through: a later interrupt descriptor returns a negative status from PciProcessInterruptDescriptor (0x1c004d001), or a subsequent MmMapIoSpaceEx returns NULL (0x1c0063813). The cleanup path unmaps device_ext+0x398 but (unpatched) leaves the pointer set. 3. Device start fails and PnP issues IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE / IRP_MN_STOP_DEVICE. 4. PciDevice_Remove / PciDevice_Stop re-read device_ext+0x388 == 3 and unmap device_ext+0x398 a second time.
security_findings.primitive: Double MmUnmapIoSpace of an IO-space system-PTE mapping. Impact is a bugcheck (denial of service) and potential kernel system-PTE accounting corruption when the start-failure path and the teardown path both unmap the same VA. The attack vector is a malicious or malfunctioning PCIe device that triggers an error during MSI-X resource processing, or a device hot-plug/remove sequence. No demonstrated controlled read/write primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
PciProcessStartResources map + cleanup (unpatched 0x1C004CE40):
1C004D0C3: mov     r13d, [rdx+414h]              ; BAR count
1C004D0D8: shl     r13d, 4                        ; size = BAR_count * 16
1C004D0DF: call    cs:__imp_MmMapIoSpaceEx        ; map physical IO space
1C004D0EB: mov     [rbp+10h], rax                 ; store VA into device_ext+0x398
1C004D0F2: je      1C0063813                      ; fail path
1C0063818: mov     rcx, [rsp+arg_20]              ; arg5 = device_ext+0x388
1C0063820: cmp     dword ptr [rcx], 3
1C0063829: mov     rcx, [rcx+10h]                 ; BaseAddress
1C0063839: call    cs:__imp_MmUnmapIoSpace
1C0063845: nop                                    ; *** pointer left set (unpatched) ***
1C0063846: jmp     1C004CF6C

breakpoints.why: Entry of the resource descriptor processor. RCX=arg1 (device extension), RDX=arg2 (resource list), R9=arg4 (output buffer), arg5 (stack) = device_ext+0x388 (the persistent MSI-X descriptor). The function iterates resource descriptors and calls PciProcessInterruptDescriptor for MSI-X processing.

MmUnmapIoSpace on the start-failure cleanup path. In the unpatched build device_ext+0x398 is still set to the unmapped VA after this returns; in the patched build 0x14009c72d writes 0 to it. Set a breakpoint here and single-step to confirm the missing clear.

Teardown path that re-unmaps device_ext+0x398 if non-NULL (checks device_ext+0x388 == 3). With the pointer left stale from a failed start, this performs the SECOND MmUnmapIoSpace on the same VA. PciDevice_Stop (0x1c006cae0) has the same pattern.

watch: arg5 = device_ext+0x388 (MSI-X descriptor); device_ext+0x398 = arg5+0x10 = the mapped BaseAddress
After the unpatched cleanup: device_ext+0x398 is STILL the old mapped VA (patched: 0)
On teardown: PciDevice_Remove/PciDevice_Stop unmap device_ext+0x398 if non-NULL -> second unmap if left stale
key_offsets: 0x1c004d0df: call MmMapIoSpaceEx - maps the MSI-X table BAR
0x1c004d0eb: mov [rbp+0x10], rax - stores VA into device_ext+0x398
0x1c0063839: call MmUnmapIoSpace - unmaps on the start-failure path
PciDevice_Remove 0x1c0010340 / PciDevice_Stop 0x1c006cae0 - teardown re-unmap of device_ext+0x398
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Trigger via PCI device lifecycle: (1) Attach a PCIe device with MSI-X capability. (2) Let PnP start the device (IRP_MN_START_DEVICE) so the MSI-X table BAR is mapped (device_ext+0x398 set). (3) Force an error during MSI-X processing after the mapping - e.g. a later resource descriptor causes PciProcessInterruptDescriptor to return a negative status (0x1c004d001), or a subsequent MmMapIoSpaceEx returns NULL (0x1c0063813) - so the cleanup path unmaps device_ext+0x398 but leaves it set. (4) Device start fails; PnP issues IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE / IRP_MN_STOP_DEVICE, whose teardown re-unmaps device_ext+0x398 a second time.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Two MmUnmapIoSpace calls with the same base address: one on the start-failure cleanup path, one during PciDevice_Remove / PciDevice_Stop teardown. The second call unmaps an already-freed system-PTE range, leading to a bugcheck or system-PTE accounting corruption. Breakpoint MmUnmapIoSpace and compare the base address across the two calls.
debugging_aids.notes: The MSI-X descriptor (arg5 = device_ext+0x388, 0x68 bytes) layout: +0x00 dword type (3=MSI-X), +0x08 byte BIR/BAR index, +0x0C dword table offset, +0x10 qword BaseAddress (the dangling pointer = device_ext+0x398), +0x18 dword vector index/count, +0x20 qword BAR-to-vector table pool, +0x28 qword MSI-X descriptor copy pool. The teardown unmap uses size 16 * BAR_count (device_ext+0x414 unpatched / +0x524 patched). Pool tag 0x42696350 ('PciB').

summary: The patch makes two defensive changes in the Windows PCI bus driver's (pci.sys) MSI-X interrupt-resource processing path, both reachable only during PnP device start / enumeration on the device's own PCI configuration space (i.e. by a malicious or malfunctioning PCIe device, including a VM PCI-passthrough / SR-IOV guest), not from an untrusted IOCTL surface. First, PciProcessInterruptDescriptor now reads the device's MSI-X capability from config space BEFORE allocation and validates that the capability ID byte is 0x11 (MSI-X) and the table BIR field (& 7) is not 7, returning STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST (0xC0000184) otherwise; the unpatched version read the capability after allocation and used the device-supplied BIR and table offset unchecked (CWE-20). There is NO integer overflow here: the 32-bit 88*count+8 allocation-size multiply and the matched memmove length are identical in both builds and untouched by the patch. Second, PciProcessStartResources now clears the stored IO-space mapping pointer (device_ext+0x398) after MmUnmapIoSpace on the error path; the unpatched version left it stale, so the persistent descriptor caused the teardown paths PciDevice_Remove (0x1c0010340) and PciDevice_Stop (0x1c006cae0) to unmap the same VA a second time - a double MmUnmapIoSpace / expired-pointer dereference (CWE-825) leading to a bugcheck or system-PTE accounting corruption. An independent size-arithmetic sweep across all shared functions found no additional integer-overflow guard beyond these two changes.
